data.title: US Steel gains amid report regulators more likely to approve Nippon Steel deal
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) earlier rose as much as 4%, though has since pared its gain to 1.7%, amid a report that national security regulators may be a little more likely to approve the $14 billion sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY).

There appears to be slightly better than 50/50 odds that the deal can still be approved by the Biden administration, traders said, citing a CTFN article that was circulating on Wednesday, which cited a source with insight into the CFIUS process.

Also on Wednesday, a top Nippon Steel official reiterated to news outlets, including Bloomberg and Reuters, that he's confident the deal can close even as it sees opposition from the current and incoming US presidents.

"We are almost 100% sure that we can close the deal by the end of the year," Takahiro Mori, vice president of Japan’s Nippon Steel said, according to Reuters.

Mori's comments come after US Steel (X) shares plunged 8% on Tuesday after President-elect Donald Trump reiterated that he would block the US Steel (X) deal.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) in September was allowed to withdrawal and refile its submission to CFIUS. The extension added three months and pushed the review past the U.S. election. CFIUS is expected to make a decision on the deal later this month.

data.title: UnitedHealth insurance CEO Brian Thompson killed in 'premeditated' shooting
data.description:
Editor's note: This article has been clarified that Thompson was CEO of the company's health benefits unit. UnitedHealth Group CEO Andrew Witty is unharmed. In addition, the company's investor day has been cancelled. The article also includes additional details from a late morning NYPD press conference.

UnitedHealthcare (NYSE:UNH) CEO Brian Thompson was shot and killed earlier Wednesday morning outside a midtown Manhattan hotel where he was scheduled to speak at the company's annual investor conference.

Thompson -- who was the CEO Of UnitedHealth Group's health benefits unit, UnitedHealthcare -- was shot at least twice by a masked man with a backpack just outside the New York Hilton Midtown around 646a local time, NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch said at a late morning news conference. The alleged assailant then fled down 6th Ave. on a bicycle and has yet to be captured. She added the suspect, a white male, was last seen possibly in Central Park.

Tisch confirmed that Thompson was targeted as eyewitnesses told police that the man was seen for some time waiting outside the hotel. "This was a premediatated, preplanned, targeted attack."

Thompson was at the hotel as the health insurer's annual investor conference was slated to start at 8a. That event has been cancelled, CNBC reported.

In a phone interview with NBC News, Paulette Thompson said that her husband had been receiving threats recently. "Basically, I don’t know, a lack of coverage? I don’t know details. I just know that he said there were some people that had been threatening him."

UnitedHeathcare media relations has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha seeking comment on the killing. Thompson's bio page on the company's website has been removed.

Thompson first joined UnitedHealth Group in 2004 and became CEO of the UnitedHealthcare division in April 2021. He was 50.

UnitedHealth Group shares opened up ~2% Wednesday.

data.title: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman says Elon Musk wouldn't use political clout against rivals - report
data.description:
OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman said that the next major developments in the AI space will be more disruptive than people expect, and that he does not think Elon Musk would use his political power with Donald Trump to harm rivals, Variety reported.

Altman was speaking at the New York Times' DealBook Summit in New York City on Wednesday. He noted that as soon as 2025 the industry will start to see the first examples of artificial general intelligence, or AGI, in which one can give an AI system a complicated task, and it will use different tools to complete it, the report added.

At first, the introduction of AGI — or "superintelligence" as some define it — will have minimal effect, said Altman. However, eventually, it would "be more intense than people think," said Altman adding that with every major technological development, there has been significant job displacement.

When asked about critics who say OpenAI is not focused enough on safety, Altman noted that ChatGPT "is now generally considered by most of society to be acceptably safe and acceptably robust." He added that while "there are definitely people who think ChatGPT is not sufficiently safe" the company believes that iterative deployment is vital and that "you have to start when the stakes are lower."

Altman also talked about tech tycoon Elon Musk. Tesla CEO Musk was among the co-founders of OpenAI — which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) — along with Altman. Musk has sued OpenAI and Altman, alleging breach of contract by deviating from its original nonprofit mission.

"I grew up seeing Elon as a mega-hero," said Altman. "At some point [Musk] totally lost faith in OpenAI," he added.

Altman also said he thinks Musk's own AI startup xAI will be a serious competitor.

In addition, Altman noted that he does not think Musk would use his political clout with Donald Trump to harm competitors, saying that such behavior would be "profoundly un-American."

In October, OpenAI raised about $6.6N in new funding from investors including Microsoft and Nvidia (NVDA) giving it a valuation of $157B.

On OpenAI's collaboration with Microsoft, Altman noted that it has not been without "misalignments or challenges" but added that on the whole it has been a "positive thing for both companies."

Altman said, "there's not no tension, but on the whole our priorities are aligned," as per the report.

Altman also said that ChatGPT now has over 300 million users per week, up from the 200 million weekly users in August, The Verge reported.

data.title: Coveo downgraded at Bank of America on demand challenges, rising competition
data.description:
• Coveo (OTCPK:CVOSF) (TSX:CVO:CA) was downgraded by Bank of America on Wednesday on what the investment firm called "demand challenges" and rising competition.
  • "Generative-AI search software vendor Coveo is challenged by increasing budget scrutiny with existing enterprise customers, coupled with increasing competition from start-ups and incumbent software vendors," Bank of America analyst Koji Ikeda wrote in a note to clients. "We think management is making smart long-term moves by investing for growth in one of the most attractive trends in technology, generative AI. However, it may likely cap free cash flow upside near-term."
  • Ikeda lowered his rating on Coveo to Underperform from Neutral and lowered his price target to $7 CAD from $9 CAD.
  • As generative AI continues to evolve, it's possible that it will be harder for Coveo to take share and with the stock up 43% since the company's second-quarter results, shares could "relatively underperform until investors see more tangible share gains," Ikeda explained.

data.title: Alector downgraded to Underperform at BofA after Alzheimer's trial miss
data.description:
• As previously reported, BofA downgraded Alector (NASDAQ:ALEC) to Underperform from Neutral with a price target of $1, down from $9, after ALOO2 missed the mark in its Phase 2 Alzheimer's trial. Shares trades 10% lower.
  • The firm sees this as likely the end for the AL002 program, does not expect the $250M opt-in milestone from AbbVie (ABBV) to be realized, and removed AL002 from its model, the analyst tells investors.
  • The firm also decreased its estimate of the value of AL001 and sees "significant investment" needed to spur the next leg of pipeline growth.
  • Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, ALEC has a score just 1.27 out of 5 and sees the stock as a “Strong Sell”. The company received D- in the prospect of profitability and momentum, while it got a B- in valuation and C in growth factors.
  • Turning to the Wall Street community, just 6 analysts gave ALEC a Strong Buy while just 2 stood with Buy whereas, 1 of them gave the stock a Hold recommendation, and 2 were on the short side.
  • Seeking Alpha analysts see the stock as a Sell, with a rating of 2.00.

data.title: Thor Industries' profit evaporates on tough RV market, initiatives to streamline business
data.description:
Thor Industries (NYSE:THO) swung to a loss in the fiscal first quarter as the challenging sales environment coupled with strategic initiatives to streamline and flatten the organization translated into a loss of profitability for the quarter. Although Thor’s (THO) profit margin was mostly insulated, the company reported a loss of $0.03 per share from a profit of $0.99 per share a year ago, missing the consensus estimate for a profit of $0.72 per share.

“As we forecasted, our performance through the first quarter of our fiscal year 2025 continued to be impacted by the soft retail and wholesale environment, said CEO Bob Martin, adding that the company’s strategic approach continues to focus on aligning production to match the current retail environment and avoiding growth of independent dealer inventory levels of our products until market conditions indicate otherwise. Martin believes this will leave the company well positioned to outperform when demand picks up.

The loss was largely a result of a steep drop in higher-ticket motorized RV sales, which were down 29% year-over-year, resulting in a 46.2% decrease in gross profit. By comparison, sales of towable RVs were down a more modest 4.9%, resulting in a 4.7% drop in profitability.

European sales were also soft, with total sales falling 14.6% and profits down 24.6%.

Looking ahead to fiscal year 2025, the company’s outlook remains consistent with its initial forecast. In terms of sequence of performance, the second quarter is expected to be equally challenging, followed by a stronger third and fourth quarter. By the end of 2025, Thor Industries (THO) anticipates that the retail market will begin to trend positively, setting up fiscal 2026 to be a stronger year. Sales in FY25 are expected to be between $9.0B and $9.8B and earnings of $4.00 to $5.00 per share. This compares to estimates of $9.61B and $4.86, respectively. Gross margin is targeted for 14.7% to 15.2%.

As a result of Thor Industries’ (THO) disappointing results and downbeat assessment of the RV industry, shares of Winnebago Industries (WGO) and LCI Industries (LCII) are trading lower in sympathy.

data.title: Schneider Electric, Nvidia introduce AI data center cooling system design
data.description:
Schneider Electric (OTCPK:SBGSF) has collaborated with AI giant Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) for a new AI data center cooling and infrastructure design.

The two have introduced a co-developed data center reference design that will support liquid-cooled, high-density AI clusters of up to 132 kW per server rack. The design will be used in Nvidia’s (NVDA) GB200 NVL72 and Blackwell chips.

The designs will be customizable for cloud computing and data center customers. Financial details around the partnership remain undisclosed.

Schneider Electric also launched its new Galaxy VXL scalable, 1.25 MW modular UPS for AI, data center, and large-scale electrical workloads.

data.title: New Fortress Energy surges as Deutsche Bank upgrades on stronger liquidity
data.description:
New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) +5.8% in Wednesday's trading as Deutsche Bank upgrades to Hold from Sell with an $11 price target, raised from $7.60, saying the company has de-risked its "near-term liquidity and debt maturity situation via a recent equity raise and debt refinancing agreement."

"Potential positive headline risks include full or partial asset sales which results in cash proceeds being available for de-levering, volumes upside growth driven by geopolitical decision making in Puerto Rico, near-term strength in the LNG spot cargo market, and volumes upside in Brazil related to new power or industrial-use customers," Deutsche Bank analyst Christopher Robertson writes.

Stifel analysts led by Benjamin Nolan also tapped New Fortress (NFE) as "clearly the biggest winner" from rising liquefied natural gas prices, as 15%-30% of the company's 2025 LNG exposure is market priced, and every $1/MMBtu move in LNG prices translates into a $25-$50 move in EBITDA.

The recent improvement could not have come at a more opportune time for the company, which is laboring to improve the capital structure and high-cost debt, Stifel says.

data.title: ClearSign awarded purchase order from Rogue Combustion
data.description:
• ClearSign Technologies (NASDAQ:CLIR) has received a purchase order from Rogue Combustion, a subsidiary of California Boiler, for a boiler burner for use in their rental fleet.
  • This purchase order came from Cal Boiler and is expected to be fabricated and shipped in the second quarter of 2025 for installation in a new rental boiler.
  • The deployment of rental boilers also provides customers with experience using their equipment, and believes that this exposure can assist in making sales.
  • A recent demonstration of the value of rental boilers was the order for a boiler burner we announced on Nov. 12 which was preceded by the deployment of a Cal Boiler rental boiler to that customer.

data.title: GSK in agreement with Rgenta to develop RNA-targeted small molecules
data.description:
• Privately held Rgenta Therapeutics has signed an agreement with GSK (NYSE:GSK) for multi-target strategic research to discover RNA-targeted small molecule splice modulators oncology and other disease areas.
  • Rgenta will use its proprietary discovery platform to develop oral RNA-targeting small molecule splice modulators against multiple targets selected by GSK.
  • Terms call for Rgenta to receive up to $46M in upfront and pre-option milestone payments and up to ~$500M per target in milestone payments as well as tiered royalties and a future equity investment.
  • GSK can also extend the agreement to include additional targets. If GSK exercises any of its options, it will be responsible for development and commercialization of any drug candidates.
  • Rgenta's lead candidate, RGT-61159 is an oral drug designed to modulate splicing of the transcription factor MYB, a mutated gene know to cause cancer. It is in phase 1a/b for potential use in adenoid cystic carcinoma, colorectal cancer, acute myeloid leukemia, and other solid tumors.
